title: Doubt In The Present
text:
Late at night. Your room is quiet. A half-read Bible sits on your desk, a coffee gone cold beside it.

You close it and stare at the ceiling.

"If I could just have been there," you think. "If I could have seen him with my own eyes, I wouldn't keep second-guessing everything. I wouldn't have these doubts."

And then you look at the device. The prototype your colleague dared you to test. The one that is — impossibly — supposed to bridge time.

A single destination sits already entered in the console: Jerusalem. 30 AD.

Do you go?

choices.text: Yes. I need to see for myself. No more doubts from a distance.
I hesitate — but doubt is exactly why I have to go.
